reviews.review_text: It is a very good game for those who want a big network rather than very extreme detail.

This is almost a twin of Microsoft Train Simulator, but the graphics are more realistic there are people in the trains and platforms and the drivers in the engines and trains are visible, it's a great find
outdated but still classic game fun train game great for entry level pcs like mine i like this game i recomend

Great game! it's been out for a while now. Modding community is great, and it's easy to run. The only thing i don't like about it is the physics. it's not very realistic in terms of physics, or graphics, but that makes it easy and fun to play when you want to wind down. I wish the physics were better, but I play it and love it for the VAST set of routes and locomotives. This is the only game that has my favorite locomotive, the SD60E. no other games, other then Trainz has the SD60E. Truly awesome.
Performance is still awful, even in medium settings. It's very unoptimized still, so beware.
Other than having to put allot of payware for more content, great game.

Good game overall, editor could be better, very confusing, mind you I am used to Trainz! 
I'd love to play the new versions but I simply cannot afford them.
not worth it, not very realistic, bought a pack and never got it in game.
Very good and easy-to-use interface, plenty of good content and not too demanding in terms of hardware requirements. Your one-stop title for instantly relax, unwind and feel good!

it's fun playing with trains, and i like how interactive the game is. There are a lot of trains in the work shop and the main game. If i added some thing it would be to make it easier to make a train.
Love this game especially with ATS mods makes it 10x better, wish TSW had half the mods available on TSC

I would advice you guys strongly to not buy this, at this time. Of course, if anything changes, I would change my review accordingly. 

The PC configuration I have.... Is very strong, let's say. It's not just RTX 5090, it has RTX 5070Ti. 
I did attempted DirectX 12. The fact that it is still experimental in 2026 is not good advertising, neither good experience for someone like me. I mean, I am on latest version of Windows 11, with DirectX 12 nonstop working here. I don't want to give up this software so easily. 

Even my flight simulator, Prepar3D v4 runs better. In this game I face often severe performance issues and bugs on occasion. 

The basic x64 version, maintained at the moment is very low hardware DirectX, not sure is it 9 or 10. However, this causes black screen the minute I click on my second screen to start recording the game and make a video out of a scenario I play, because I have small YouTube and I want to share my gameplay there. Usually this means I can't use the software for recording with your game at once, which means, I can only enjoy the game, privately, can't stream it, or create videos with it. And this is because my hardware is too new. I want to mention as well, that the startup of the game is unusual, usually takes time till I reach strartup screen and until then my desktop freezes. So, not only the non-recording is problem, by my opinion - the old architecture is also to blame. 

The User interface needs upgrade. The graphics remind me a little of my flight simulator software, but the level of detail is much less lower. Even with addon work it is difficult to remove certain graphical issues, like tint for example, yellow one or blue. I attempted Reshade, but in Prepar3D a number of freeware tweaks exist to make the game look better. Here even with high level of detail and external addons the game still looks pale in certain scenarios, mainly, interest of "Semmeringbahn" one. There is a bug there with the brake system downhill on "My Favorite things" scenario, that makes the electricity circle around the 0 to -69 amps, making noise, that is unpleasant to listen to downhill. I did attempt multiple times to find out why, I was reading manual, I was watching videos as well. It did not helped, unfortunately. 

The game gathers dust in my library most of the time. I can't afford the cost to buy all of the addon for it, neither I have time or money to invest into development for it. 

What I am asking sincerely - please, modernize this software you create, Dovetail. Because I do not have funds for your newer simulators at the moment. I want this potential to be used for a greater good. As a paying customer. Thank you.
Frustrated, spent half an hour on a tedious tutorial that barely taught anything and now I can't even get the trains to turn on/start moving

I've had this game for some time, but not played it that often.
the reason being that the graphics suck.
I have a high end gaming pc so that's not the reason, it must be something to do with the game graphics.

do not recommend the price is outrageous. And all the DLC Prices are outrageous.
